MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, PROOF OF SERVICE, DECLARATION, SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE OF BOARD OF EQUALIZATION,AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA HEARING SET FOR JUN-09-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF'S TO RESPOND TO DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S SECOND SET OF DISCOVERY AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. COURT MADE A PERSONAL DISCLOSURE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF EXHAUSTED ITS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CONTRACT WAS NOT A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT. EDS WAS NOT MAKING OR SELLING A PRODUCT RELYING ON PLAINTIFF'S PATENTS OR COPYRIGHTS. SEE PRESTON V. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 197. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO A REFUND BECAUSE IT CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT ITS CONTRACT IS A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT AND FALLS UNDER THE R&T 6011(C)(10) EXCEPTION. PLAINTIFF'S COMMERCE CLAUSE ARGUMENT FAILS BECAUSE THE SHIPPING OF THE SOFTWARE TO PLEASANTON SHOWS A SUBSTANTIAL NEXUS WITH CALIFORNIA, THE TAX IS FAIRLY APPORTIONED BASED ON THE CONTRACT AMOUNT, THERE IS NO DISCRIMINATION AGAINST INTERSTATE COMMERCE, AND IT IS FAIRLY RELATED TO THE SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE STATE. COURT OVERRULED DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS; COURT DID CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE. ORDER ON OBJECTIONS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ORDER ON MOTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-17-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: James P. Kleier, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP 659-5987 for pltf & opposing party; Joyce E. Hee, Deputy Atty General 510/622-2227 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Court made a personal disclosure. No objections were made. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF EXHAUSTED ITS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CONTRACT WAS NOT A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT. EDS WAS NOT MAKING OR SELLING A PRODUCT RELYING ON PLAINTIFF'S PATENTS OR COPYRIGHTS. SEE PRESTON V. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 197. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO A REFUND BECAUSE IT CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT ITS CONTRACT IS A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT AND FALLS UNDER THE R&T 6011(c)(10) EXCEPTION. PLAINTIFF'S COMMERCE CLAUSE ARGUMENT FAILS BECAUSE THE SHIPPING OF THE SOFTWARE TO PLEASANTON SHOWS A SUBSTANTIAL NEXUS WITH CALIFORNIA, THE TAX IS FAIRLY APPORTIONED BASED ON THE CONTRACT AMOUNT, THERE IS NO DISCRIMINATION AGAINST INTERSTATE COMMERCE, AND IT IS FAIRLY RELATED TO THE SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE STATE. Court overruled defendant's objections; Court did consider the evidence. Order on objections signed in open court. Order on motion signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Deft' Second Set Of Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And To Impose Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joyce E. Hee, Deputy Attorney General, for defendant and moving party: Defendant State Board Of Equalization

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant State Board Of Equalization's motion for order that the truth of matters specified in defendant's second set of request for admission be deemed admitted, granted. The matters set forth in defendant's second set of request for admission are deemed admitted;
2. Request for sanctions with regard to motion for order that the truth of matter specified in defendant's second set of request for admission be deemed admitted, granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,040.00, payable within thirty days of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Response To Second Set Of Form And Special Interrogatories, And Second Set Of Demands For Production Of Documents, And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joyce E. Hee, Deputy Attorney General, for defendant and moving party: Defendant State Board Of Equalization

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
3. Defendant State Board Of Equalization's motion for order to compel responses to second set of form and special interrogatories, and second set of demands for production of documents granted, as follows:
a. All objections, except attorney-client privilege and attorney work product privilege are waived;
b. Plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendant's second set of form interrogatories and special interrogatories, and second set of demands for production within five days of the date of service of this order;
4. Request for sanctions with regard to motion for order to compel responses to second set of form and special interrogatories, and second set of demands for production of documents, granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $1,040.00, payable within thirty days of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Response To Form And Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents, And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joyce E. Hee, Deputy Attorney General, for defendant and moving party: State of Board of Equalization

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
1. Defendant State Board Of Equalization's Motion To Compel Response To Form And Special Interrogatories, Request For Production granted, as follows:
a. All objections, except attorney-client privilege and attorney work product privilege are waived;
b. Plaintiff shall serve full, complete and verified responses to defendant's Form Interrogatories [Set One], Special Interrogatories [Set One], and Request For Production [Set One] within seven days of the date of this order;
c. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $790.00, payable within thirty days of the date of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Deft'S Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted, And To Impose Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Oct-23-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Joyce E. Hee, Deputy Attorney General, for defendant and moving party: State of Board of Equalization

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
2. Defendant State Board Of Equalization's motion that truth of matters specified in defendant's Request For Admission be deemed admitted granted in part, denied in part, as follows:
a. The truth of the matters specified in defendant's Request For Admission [Set One], nos. 1 through 4, inclusive, and 9 through 15, inclusive, is deemed admitted;
b. Motion as to truth of matters specified in defendant's Request For Admission [Set One], nos. 5, 6, 7, & 8, denied;
c. Request for sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue in the sum of $790.00, payable within thirty days of the date of this order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.
